title: Health Insurance After a Death: Canceling and Transferring Coverage
summary: Health insurance changes need attention fairly promptly, especially for a surviving spouse or dependents who were covered under the same plan.
category: After a Loss II: Notifications & Accounts
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Health Insurance After a Death: Canceling and Transferring Coverage | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: Health insurance changes need attention fairly promptly, especially for a surviving spouse or dependents who were covered under the same plan.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Employer-sponsored coverage for dependents may end automatically, but continuation options usually exist.
COBRA can extend employer coverage temporarily, at a cost.
Medicare enrollment timing matters if a surviving spouse is nearing 65.

paragraphs: If the deceased carried employer health coverage for a spouse or dependents, that coverage often ends automatically, but continuation options like COBRA are generally available for a limited period, at the family's cost.

For a surviving spouse approaching or at Medicare eligibility age, understanding enrollment windows matters, since missing certain deadlines can result in penalties — worth addressing this fairly soon rather than assuming there's no rush.

nextSteps: Contact the employer's HR department about continuation coverage options.
Ask specifically about COBRA deadlines, which are time-sensitive.
Check Medicare enrollment timing if a surviving spouse is nearing 65.
disclaimer: General information — the employer's benefits office or Medicare directly can confirm specific timelines and options.
